sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text:
The purpose of this standard is to define the quality requirements for yac on at the export-control stage, after preparation and packaging. However, if applied at stages following packaging, products may show, in relation to the requirements of the standard: 
 a slight lack of freshness and turgidity; 
 for products graded in classes other than the extra class, a slight deterioration due to their development and tendency to perish. 
The holder/seller of products may not display such products or offer them for sale or deliver or market them in any manner other than in conformity with this standard. The holder/seller shall be responsible for observing such conformity.

sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This standard applies to the tuberous roots of varieties and/or commercial types of yacon grown from Smallanthus sonchifolius, (Poepp. & Endl.) H. Robinson, from the Asteraceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer after preparation and packaging. Yacon for industrial processing is excluded.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING
sections.text:
The yacon may be sized by the maximum diameter of the equatorial section of each root (thicker cross section) and by the weight of each root, in accordance with existing trade practices. The package must be labelled with the corresponding size according to the following table which is a guide and may be used on an optional basis.
Size code Larger diameter (mm) Weight (g)
1 > 70  > 300
2 > 50 to 70 > 120 to 300
3 30 to 50 ≤ 120
All sizes may have the following forms: fusiform, oval or ovate, obovate and irregular, because some yacon cultivars tend to form more smooth and symmetrical than other roots.

sections.title: Class I
sections.retrieval_text:
3 > 3.2 > Class I
number=3.2.2
level=3
The yacon in this class must be of good quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package:
 slight defects in shape;
 scarring or healed damage, not exceeding ten percent of the surface area;
 scraped areas, not exceeding ten percent of the product surface.
The defects must not, in any case, affect the pulp of the product.

sections.title: Class II
sections.text:
This class includes yacon that do not qualify for inclusion in the higher classes, but satisfy the minimum requirements specified in Section 3.1 above. The following defects may be allowed, provided the yacon retains the essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation:
 defects in shape;
 scarring or healed damage, not exceeding 20 percent of the surface area; and
 scraped areas, not exceeding 20 percent of the product surface.
The flesh must be free of major quality defects.

sections.title: Uniformity
sections.text: The contents of each package or lot must be uniform as to the form and contain only yacon of the same origin, variety and/or commercial type, colour, quality and size. The visible part of the content of the package must be representative of the entire content. However, a mixture of yacon of different varieties and/or commercial types may be packaged together provided they are uniform in quality and the origin of each variety and/or commercial type is the same.

sections.title: Packaging
sections.text: Yacon must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must of food-grade quality, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or a stamps bearing trade specifications, is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue. Stickers individually affixed to the produce shall be such that, when removed, they neither leave visible traces of glue nor lead to skin defects. Yacon shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruit and Vegetables (CXC 44-1995).

classification.sentence:
The following defects may be allowed, provided the yacon retains the essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation:
 defects in shape;
 scarring or healed damage, not exceeding 20 percent of the surface area; and
 scraped areas, not exceeding 20 percent of the product surface.
The flesh must be free of major quality defects.

scraped areas, not exceeding 20 percent of the product surface
components.condition: yacon retains the essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation

classification.justification: Permission with conditions on allowed defects and quality retention; complex normative statement.

pages.title: REGIONAL STANDARD FOR YACON
pages.summary: Página com título principal e cabeçalho identificados, sem estrutura hierárquica adicional visível.
Página com um único título nível 1 "2023 Amendments" em caixa alta, que não possui numeração. Texto explicativo associado. Cabeçalho "CXS 324R-2017 2" identificado. Sem continuidade detectada.

Página com três títulos nível 1 numerados (1, 2, 3) e seus respectivos textos. O título 3 tem subtítulos nível 2 (3.1, 3.2) com texto, e estes possuem subtítulos nível 3 (3.1.1, 3.2.1). A seção 3 e subseções 3.1, 3.2, e 3.2.1 são marcadas como continuando na próxima página devido ao texto incompleto. Cabeçalho 'CXS 324R-2017 3' identificado e título principal do documento não visível.

Página contém subtítulos nível 3 (3.2.2, 3.2.3) continuando da página anterior, com texto detalhado. Há títulos nível 1 (4, 5) e subtítulos nível 2 e 3 sob o título 5, todos exibindo seus textos completos na página. Cabeçalho identificável como 'CXS 324R-2017 4', sem título principal visível.

A página 5 contém os títulos principais nível 1 numerados 6 e 7, sem título principal do documento identificado, com cabeçalho 'CXS 324R-2017 5'. Cada título nível 1 possui subtítulos nível 2 e alguns subtítulos nível 3 numerados sequencialmente conforme o texto. O texto é associado corretamente a cada nó e não indica continuidade nem se origina da página anterior. A estrutura é clara e adequada para uso em RAG.

A página apresenta três seções principais nível 1 (8, 9 e 10) com seus respectivos subtítulos nível 2 numerados. O texto explicativo está associado à seção 8. As seções 9 e 10 contém títulos subsidiários (9.1, 9.2, 10.1, 10.2) sem texto detalhado na página, indicando que são focos de requisitos normativos. Não há indicações de continuidade ou origem da página anterior. A estrutura mapeada está clara e adequada para uso em RAG.

pages.title: CXS 324R-2017
pages.summary: Página contém um único nó estrutural nível 1 'NOTES', que agrupa várias notas detalhadas numeradas de 1 a 8, além de informação adicional sobre membros da Comissão Codex. Não há subtítulos ou continuidade de outras páginas. Texto detalhado associado diretamente ao nó 'NOTES'.
